other_observers: none; I am the original finder of this bird.
specific_location: 40.051068N -105.180726E The bird was on the north side of Boulder Creek where the bike path meets the creek just west of the parking lot on the west side of 75th St at Walden Ponds.
description: The bird was roughly Common Yellowthroat sized warbler with a thin white eyering, possibly complete. The bird's throat was yellowish pale and the bird had a completely yellow belly with yellow undertail coverts. The yellow on the belly and undertail coverts were brighter than the yellow on the throat. The birds back was olive and the birds wings were unmarked. The bird was not distinctly hooded.Behaviors: The bird was feeding in a willow thicket next to Boulder Creek. The bird never flew or fed higher than ~3 ft from the ground. The bird was calling with a chip note and responded to pishing.Call: The bird was initially located because it was calling from the willows along Boulder Creek. The call was a chip note. My initial impression was that when the bird appeared from the willows, it would be a Common Yellowthroat. The call differed from Common Yellowthroat by having a more hollow sound more easily mimicked by "sucking ones tongue". I was able to "pish" the bird out into the open by mimicking the call note. The bird responded at least twice to the pishing before it retreated further into the willows.Plumage: First Fall
